events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Richard Starkey, known professionally as Ringo Starr, was born on July 7, 1940, and rose to international fame as the drummer for The Beatles.

angles.name: Ringo's Beat
angles.copy: Happy 86th Birthday to the legendary Ringo Starr! From Abbey Road to his solo hits, his iconic drumming shaped generations.

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist, Brian Harold May, was born on July 19, 1947.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Wishing a very happy 79th birthday to Queen's iconic guitarist, Brian May! Those legendary riffs continue to rock us.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, lead singer of The Rolling Stones, was born on July 26, 1943.

angles.name: Still Got The Moves
angles.copy: Happy 83rd Birthday to the one and only Mick Jagger! The Rolling Stones frontman still has the moves that define rock and roll.

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: MTV, the first 24-hour music television channel, launched on August 1, 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its first video.

angles.name: Video Killed The Radio Star
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched with that iconic first video. It changed music forever, but don't worry, radio's still here!

events.title: Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe' anniversary
events.blurb: Oasis released their seminal debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, becoming a cornerstone of Britpop.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: Still sounds as fresh as '94. Celebrating the enduring swagger and anthems of Oasis' debut, 'Definitely Maybe'. What's your favourite track?

events.title: Woodstock Festival anniversary (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal event in music history, took place from August 15-18, 1969, in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: 57 years since half a million gathered for '3 Days of Peace & Music'. Remembering the legendary Woodstock Festival.

events.title: Elvis Presley would have been 91
events.blurb: Elvis Aaron Presley, the 'King of Rock and Roll', passed away on August 16, 1977, and would have been 91 today.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: On this day, we remember the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley. His music lives on, forever changing the sound of popular culture.

events.title: Jimi Hendrix would have been 84
events.blurb: Jimi Hendrix, widely regarded as one of the greatest guitarists of all time, passed away on September 18, 1970, and would have been 84.

angles.name: Guitar Revolutionary
angles.copy: Remembering Jimi Hendrix, a true pioneer whose revolutionary guitar playing continues to inspire. He would have been 84 today.

events.title: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' anniversary
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic 'Abbey Road' album in the UK on September 26, 1969, featuring one of music's most famous album covers.

angles.name: Iconic Crossing
angles.copy: It's been 57 years since The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. That album cover is still instantly recognisable worldwide.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Christine Ellen Hynde, lead singer of The Pretenders, was born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Pretenders Icon
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! Her distinctive voice and fierce punk spirit with The Pretenders remain timeless.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: William John Paul Gallagher, best known as the lead singer of Oasis, was born on September 21, 1972.

angles.name: Rock N Roll Star
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! Still bringing that rock 'n' roll swagger to the stage.

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' anniversary
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic single 'Imagine' was released in the UK in 1975, carrying a powerful message of peace and unity.

angles.name: A World Of Peace
angles.copy: More than 50 years on, John Lennon's 'Imagine' still resonates. A timeless message for a better world.

events.title: First iPod released (2001)
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the very first iPod on October 23, 2001, revolutionising how we listen to music by putting '1,000 songs in your pocket'.

angles.name: Music In Your Pocket
angles.copy: Remember life before? 25 years since Apple introduced the iPod, changing how we carry and consume music forever.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day celebrates the release of the first Bond film, 'Dr. No', on October 5, 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Celebrating over six decades of 007's iconic adventures, incredible gadgets, and unforgettable theme tunes.

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Winston Ono Lennon, a founding member of The Beatles and a renowned solo artist, was born on October 9, 1940, and would have been 86.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering John Lennon on what would have been his 86th birthday. His music and message of peace continue to inspire.
